articles.title: Thoma Bravo eyes stake sale in Command Alkon, sources say
articles.summary: Thoma Bravo is exploring a sale of its stake in construction software maker Command Alkon, as the private equity firm ‌tries to generate returns in pockets of software seen as more resilient to ‌broader AI disruption risks, according to four people familiar with the matter. Thoma Bravo, which jointly owns Command Alkon with German ​building materials company Heidelberg Materials, is working with investment bankers at Evercore on a sale process for its roughly 55 percent stake in Command Alkon and has been fielding interest from other private equity firms in recent weeks, the sources said.
